title: Culturing Chlamydomonas reinhardtii
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5f86","text":"This protocols describe the steps required for the growth of Chlamydomonas reinhardtii in liquid media.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":61,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5c20h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"19alm","text":"All steps described in this protocol are intended to be conducted in a research laboratory.  Follow aseptic procedures.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: TAP media or other (How to prepare TAP media here ) Erlenmeyer flask Orbital shaker Light source

*Inoculation can be perfomed by scraping an agar plate containing Chlamydomonas reinhardtii or from another liquid culture Innoculate the cells in an erlenmeyer containing media (Ex: 100 mL, Erlenmeyer flask containing 50 mL of TAP media or 500 mL, Erlenmeyer flask containing 250 mL ) Place the flask in a shaker at 150 rpm, 25 °C, with 1 cm, of orbit and with 80 μmol/m2s of incident white light ( 60 μmol/m2s to 120 μmol/m2s works ) Time to stationary phase depends on initial innoculum.
creator: Joao Vitor Molino
